one_line_takeaway: SafePlan bolts a three-layer deontic-logic prompt-sanity check plus an LTL-style invariant/pre/post-condition COT verifier onto LLM robot planners, cutting harmful-prompt acceptance by ~90% (e.g., Gemini 1.5 Pro unsafe acceptance 61.3%->7.1%) and crash rates to 0% in AI2-THOR, at the cost of rejecting up to ~40% of legitimately safe commands.
key_finding: On the 621-task benchmark, adding SafePlan sharply improves safe/unsafe discrimination. Gemini 1.5 Pro w/ SafePlan reached the highest accuracy 88.4% while accepting 70.9% of safe tasks and only 7.09% of unsafe tasks (92.9% unsafe rejection), giving an E/U ratio of 10.0 vs 1.63 for the plain baseline; GPT-4o improved to E/U 9.79 (vs 1.35) and Gemini Flash 2.0 to 7.47 (vs 1.17). The headline 90.5% reduction in harmful-prompt acceptance corresponds to Gemini 1.5 Pro's unsafe acceptance dropping from 61.34% to 7.09%. Improvements were McNemar-significant for GPT-4o (chi2=14.78, p=0.0001) and Gemini Flash 2.0 (chi2=34.83, p<0.0001) but NOT for Gemini 1.5 Pro (p=1.0). In AI2-THOR, baseline GPT-4o averaged only 22% SR (executing all tasks, 100% crash rate on Scene/Skill Reasoning), whereas SafePlan reached average SR of 84% (Gemini Flash 2.0), 95% (Gemini 1.5 Pro), and 95% (GPT-4o), driving crash rates on executed tasks to 0%.
core_contribution: SafePlan, a multi-component wrapper around off-the-shelf LLMs that adds safety screening to LLM-based robotic task planning/allocation. It has two novel reasoning components the authors build: (1) a Prompt Sanity Check chain-of-thought (COT) Reasoner that parses a natural-language prompt into actions/entities/resources/location and evaluates it through three progressive deontic-logic layers (Societal, Organizational, Individual), each emitting a Forbidden/Permissible/Obligatory label, to reject unsafe prompts BEFORE task decomposition; and (2) an Invariant COT Reasoner that uses Linear Temporal Logic to generate invariants, preconditions, and postconditions for a task, which are then fed as few-shot guidance to a second code-generating LLM and used again as an LLM-critic to verify the generated task-allocation code. The authors also contribute a 621-prompt expert-curated benchmark of prompt+scene-description pairs and an AI2-THOR simulation evaluation. NOTE: works like SELP [11], the LTL safety-chip [4], SMART-LLM [2], ProgPrompt [1], and the KGP drone-safety layer [12] are cited Related Work, not this paper's contribution; SafePlan positions itself against them by screening at the prompt-decomposition stage without fine-tuning.
threat_model: LLM-based robotics systems increasingly use LLMs as the interface that receives natural-language commands and emits task plans/allocations and executable (Python) robot code. The threat is that malicious or merely unsafe/ill-considered prompts (e.g., 'Pass the cup of hot water to the two-year-old child', or context-unsafe deliveries near vulnerable people) get executed in the physical world, causing harm. The paper argues built-in RLHF safety mechanisms are tuned for question-answering, not physical/scene-grounded safety, and that contemporary LLM-robotics work implicitly assumes prompts are already safe. This is a safety/guardrail threat model (harmful-instruction interception plus logically-inconsistent-plan interception); it is NOT an adaptive-adversary model — prompts are not crafted to evade the guard, and the guard is built from the same LLM families it screens.
limitations: 1) The safety gain comes at a real utility cost the paper underplays: SafePlan drops safe-task acceptance from 100% to 57-71% (e.g., Gemini Flash 2.0 accepts only 57.5% of safe tasks), so many benign commands are over-rejected. 2) The benchmark is described as a 'synthetic' curated set and labels safe/unsafe are equated with 'ethical/unethical', which is judgment-laden; unsafe tasks dominate (494/127), inflating accuracy for a conservative rejector. 3) Assistive/human-care tasks still let through 28.9% of unsafe prompts (Gemini 1.5 Pro). 4) The Gemini 1.5 Pro improvement is not statistically significant (p=1.0), yet it is the model used for the headline number. 5) The simulation is tiny (18 tasks, 6 per category), and SR% is a bespoke metric that credits blocking-everything (if E%=0, SR still rewards fault detection F). 6) No adaptive adversary: prompts are not designed to evade the guard, and the guard reuses the same LLM families it screens, so failure modes are correlated. 7) The 'formal logic' is operationalized as a structured LLM system prompt rather than a sound external verifier/model-checker, so guarantees are heuristic, not formally enforced.

datasets_used: SafePlan benchmark: 621 expert-curated (synthetic) prompt + scene-description task pairs; 127 safe/ethical, 494 unsafe/unethical; 3 domains (97 navigation, 456 manipulation, 68 assistive) and 3 complexity levels (188 simple, 337 medium, 96 complex)
AI2-THOR interactive simulation environment: 18 error-prone tasks, 6 each across Scene Reasoning, Skill Reasoning, and Language Ambiguity
quantitative_results: Gemini 1.5 Pro w/ SafePlan: accuracy 88.4%, safe acceptance 70.87%, unsafe acceptance 7.09%, precision 0.720, recall 0.709, F1 0.714, E/U ratio 10.0 (vs 1.63 baseline)
Gemini 1.5 Pro baseline: accuracy 51.2%, safe acceptance 100%, unsafe acceptance 61.34%, F1 0.456
GPT-4o w/ SafePlan: accuracy 87.1%, safe acceptance 61.42%, unsafe acceptance 6.28%, F1 0.661, E/U 9.79 (vs 1.35); McNemar chi2=14.78, p=0.0001
Gemini Flash 2.0 w/ SafePlan: accuracy 85.2%, safe acceptance 57.48%, unsafe acceptance 7.69%, F1 0.613, E/U 7.47 (vs 1.17); McNemar chi2=34.83, p<0.0001
Headline: 90.5% reduction in harmful task prompt acceptance while maintaining reasonable safe-task acceptance
Domain (Gemini 1.5 Pro w/ SafePlan): manipulation acc 91.7% / unsafe-accept 4.7%; navigation acc 84.5%; assistive acc 72.1% / unsafe-accept 28.9%
Complexity E/U ratios (SafePlan): Gemini Flash 2.0 reaches 43.2 on complex tasks; Gemini 1.5 Pro 12.46 (simple), 9.06 (medium), 9.72 (complex)
AI2-THOR average SR%: baseline GPT-4o 22%; Gemini Flash 2.0 w/ SafePlan 84%; Gemini 1.5 Pro w/ SafePlan 95%; GPT-4o w/ SafePlan 95%
AI2-THOR Scene Reasoning: baseline 100% execution / 100% crash / SR 0%; SafePlan intercepts most (0-16% execution), 0% crash on executed, fault detection F 84-100%
baselines_compared: Same three LLMs (GPT-4o, Gemini 2.0-flash, Gemini 1.5-Pro) with a plain natural-language safety system prompt of the type used in prior LLM-robotics work (ProgPrompt [1], SMART-LLM [2], PlanCollabNL [18])
AI2-THOR baseline: LLM-generated code executed directly in the simulator with no invariant verification
